advantages: Code mirrors the recursive structure of the problem (trees, divide-and-conquer, search spaces).
Often shorter and more readable than the iterative equivalent.
Natural fit for memoization → many DP problems are one decorator away from O(n).
Generators + recursion give clean, stream-friendly traversals.
disadvantages: Each call costs a stack frame; Python's default limit is ~1000 and there's no TCO.
Naïve recursion with overlapping sub-problems is exponential without memoization.
Bugs (missing base case, no progress) loop forever and exhaust the stack.
Harder to reason about state and timing than a plain loop.
